title: Antitrust standards & enforcement, tech repricing, lab leak obfuscation, E63 reactions & more
description:
00:00 Cold Open

00:25 Besties react to last week's All-In E63

21:50 Implications of FTC Chair Lina Khan's approach to enforcing antitrust

42:21 "Superbubble," Multiple compression, Fed interest rates, Peloton & Netflix valuations

1:11:04 Sacks explains the NIH's coordinated effort to downplay the lab leak hypothesis

1:32:01 Friedberg's new company Cana

1:33:47 Learning loss from COVID school closures

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses antitrust standards, tech repricing, the lab leak hypothesis, and reactions to their previous episode. They also touch on the implications of the FTC Chair's approach and the potential consequences of COVID-19 school closures. Additionally, they introduce David Friedberg's new company, Cana.

takeaways.text: Antitrust bills may restrict big tech platforms
COVID-19 school closures could cost students $17 trillion
The lab leak hypothesis was downplayed by the NIH
The Fed may need to raise interest rates by 50 basis points
David Friedberg's new company is Cana
Learning loss from COVID-19 school closures is a significant issue
The hosts discuss the implications of the 'superbubble' and multiple compression

sentiment.notes: The hosts express concern about the implications of antitrust bills and the potential consequences of COVID-19 school closures. They also discuss the uncertainty surrounding the lab leak hypothesis and the need for the Fed to raise interest rates.

notable_moments.description: Discussion of antitrust standards and enforcement begins
Conversation about COVID-19 school closures and learning loss starts
The lab leak hypothesis is discussed
Introduction of David Friedberg's new company, Cana
